title: tomanthea araneosa
intro: Tomanthea araneosa, also known as the "spider flower," is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Tomanthea araneosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the dried leaves and stems being used to make herbal teas.
